paper_title: Compliant directional suspensions for climbing with spines and adhesives
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/03_Compliant directional suspensions for climbing with spines and adhesives.pdf
sections_checked: Front matter, Abstract, Acknowledgments, Contents, Nomenclature

equations_checked.notes: Retained as E1; angle convention, friction term and inequality direction visually checked.
Retained as E2; Gamma density, mean and variance checked. Distribution requires target-surface refitting.
Retained as E3; angular boundary and magnitude cap separated explicitly.

equations_checked.equation: Unnumbered travel, natural-frequency, stiffness and re-engagement constraints
equations_checked.notes: Retained as E4-E5. The printed re-engagement approximation omits the negative sign required by the surrounding text and Table 3.1; card records kxy<0 and the magnitude separately.

equations_checked.equation: Unnumbered stalk kinematics and force decomposition
equations_checked.notes: Retained as E6; geometry, signs and force components checked against Fig. 4.5. Implementation should use atan2.
Retained as E7; maximum-to-average attached-spine force ratio and angle-loss definition checked.
Checked as intermediate patch stiffness/moment derivations; assumptions and cascade mechanism retained without duplicating algebra.
Retained as E8; moment balance and edge-load limit visually checked. Applies to a continuous adhesive patch, not directly to discrete paired claws.
Failure branches and quadratic size trend retained; exact Hertz expressions omitted because the effective elastic quantity and final coefficient are dimensionally/algebraically suspect.
The printed Rq expression lacks 1/N and must not be implemented as written.

tables_checked.notes: Surface-measurement and asperity-density ranges checked; only parameters needed for the mechanism card retained.
State-dependent constraints checked; confirms kxy<0 during re-engagement.
Stalk parameters retained. Printed kext unit is N m; definition, Fig. 4.2 and dimensional analysis require N/m.

figures_checked.reason: Defines the finite-tip swept volume, traced surface, approach angle and usable ledge regions; crop visually verified.
Simulation trends, surface rankings and limit-curve tests are retained in formulas, parameters and V1-V2.
State and stiffness constraints are compactly represented by M3 and E4-E5.
Irreplaceable geometry for the extensional/torsional stalk decomposition; crop visually verified.
Model comparison and load-sharing outcomes are retained numerically and structurally in V3-V4 and E7.
Direct force-history evidence for alternating detachment/re-engagement while total adhesion remains nonzero; crop visually verified.
Experimental setup and remaining trial traces are summarized in V5; Fig. 4.27 is the minimal retained visual.
Continuous-patch moment mechanism is adequately represented by E8 and is only indirectly transferable to discrete spine arrays.

omitted_sections: General climbing-robot taxonomy and application background not affecting the retained mechanisms
Detailed manufacturing narrative for earlier robot generations
Intermediate algebra already represented by final constraints
Full dry-adhesive constitutive derivation outside the discrete-spine scope

uncertain_items.detail: Text and Table 3.1 require kxy<0, but the following magnitude approximation is printed without a minus sign.
The printed interval 50°<theta_max<20° is impossible; only its qualitative intermediate-angle meaning is retained.
The table prints N m, whereas the definition, Fig. 4.2 and dimensions indicate N/m.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.145 / printed p.130, Appendix A.1
uncertain_items.detail: The effective elastic quantity is defined as a compliance sum but then used as a modulus; the final coefficient is not fully self-consistent.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.149 / printed p.134, Appendix A.3
uncertain_items.detail: The printed square-root expression omits the 1/N term required for RMS roughness.

known_warnings: The surface-engagement model is a quasi-static two-dimensional profile model and excludes lateral slip, undercuts and dynamic bouncing.
Most quantitative evidence comes from sandpaper, tarpaper, stone or concrete and must be recalibrated for fired-clay brick.
Chapter 5 primarily models a continuous dry-adhesive patch under a rigid-backing, planar, small-angle assumption; it is not a paired-claw model.
The dissertation contains five internal print/dimensional inconsistencies listed under uncertain_items; none is silently corrected or used as a direct parameter source.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the dissertation contains five independent mechanism groups.
